chapter_title.en: Weeping of the Heavens and the Earth over the Killing of Ĥusain (a.s.) and Yaĥyā ibn Zakariyyā (a.s.)

text: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو مَعْشَرٍ عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ لَمَّا قُتِلَ الْحُسَيْنُ (ع) أَمْطَرَتِ السَّمَاءُ دَماً وَ قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ وَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو مَعْشَرٍ عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ لَمَّا قُتِلَ الْحُسَيْنُ (ع) لَمْ يَبْقَ فِي بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ حَصَاةٌ إِلَّا وُجِدَ تَحْتَهَا دَمٌ عَبِيطٍ

en.sayyid-mohsen-al-husayni-al-milani: 20. (Muḥammad ibn Ja’far Al-Razzaz narrated to me from Muḥammad ibn Ḥusain ibn Abil Khaťťāb), from Nasr ibn Muzāḥim, from ‘Amr ibn Sa’īd, from Abī Ma’shar (Yūsuf ibn Yazīd Al-Barrā), from (Ibn Shihāb) Al-Zuhri, who said: When Ḥusain (a.s.) was killed, the heavens rained blood and no stone was removed in Bayt Al-Maqdis without (one) seeing fresh blood under it.
